chunks.text: Constitution Petition No. S-116 of 2026 Date Order with Signature of the Judge 1.For orders on CMA No.2278/2026 2.For orders on office objections 1 to 4 at ‘A’. 3.For hearing of main case. 20th April, 2026. Mr. Jawwad Dero, Advocate General Sindh along with Mr. Muhammad Kamran Baloch, A.A.G.Sindh. Ghazanfar Ali Dahri, Dy.Secretary BOR Sindh Respondent No.1 present in person. >>>>>> <<<<<< The matter was fixed for hearing today and respondent No.1/ Ghulam Akbar Khan Jatoi, who is also a practicing advocate, disturbed the Court proceedings by shouting and use of abusive language for the petitioners. Respondent No.1 in open Court issued threats to the Assistant Advocate General, Sindh and the officials appearing on behalf of petitioners/departments saying that he will see them outside the Court. It appears that Respondent No.1 by hook or crook intends to pressurize the petitioners to withdraw this claim. It is duty of Respondent No.1, who claims to be a lawyer to maintain decorum allowing the Court to dispense with justice. Conduct of Respondent No.1 is against professional ethics and etiquettes and stand noble profession. Use of abusive language threats or insulting the oponents during court proceedings is an attempt to ridiculize and scandalize the Court in performance of due judicial process. Conduct of the Respondent No.1 is defiance of the Court’s decorum and is an act of contempt punishable under the provisions of Contempt of Court Ordinance, 2003. In the circumstance, let show cause notice be issued to respondent No.1 namely Ghulam Akbar Khan Jatoi son of Tharo Khan, who himself is an Advocate, as to why, in view of above conduct, you/Ghulam Akbar Khan Jatoi

Conduct of the Respondent No.1 is defiance of the Court’s decorum and is an act of contempt punishable under the provisions of Contempt of Court Ordinance, 2003. In the circumstance, let show cause notice be issued to respondent No.1 namely Ghulam Akbar Khan Jatoi son of Tharo Khan, who himself is an Advocate, as to why, in view of above conduct, you/Ghulam Akbar Khan Jatoi 2 son of Tharo Khan may not be prosecuted for having committed criminal and judicial contempt of Court in terms of Contempt of Court Ordinance, 2003 read with Article 204 of the Constitution of Pakistan, 1973, as well as for misconduct in terms of the Legal Practitioners and Bar Council Act, 1973. The respondent No.1/ Ghulam Akbar Khan Jatoi son of Tharo Khan is directed to show cause and submit his reply before the next date of hearing in writing without fail. Office is directed to communicate copy of this order to the Secretary, Sindh Bar Council and President, High Court Bar Association Karachi for information, who shall dealt with the matter in accordance with law. To come up on 04.05.2026. JUDGE Aamir/PS Head of Const:Benches
